purpose: Score Galaxy Guardians against local playtest perception of Galaxian-like sound, pace, and visual identity, separate from evidence coverage. This artifact intentionally lowers the apparent preview score where the current build is measurable but not yet compelling.

sourceEvidence.playtestContext: Local browser review of the development-only Galaxy Guardians preview on the MacBook workstation.
playtestFindings: Sound is nothing like the original Galaxians.
Pace feels off and slow in various ways.
Graphics still feel pretty far from the original.

metricScale.0: No recognizable Galaxian relationship in play.
metricScale.5: Recognizable reference intent exists, but the player still reads it as a rough approximation.
metricScale.7: Compelling preview: a player immediately understands the Galaxian family resemblance even before final extraction.
metricScale.10: Reference-faithful enough for public conformance claims.

summary.releaseRead: Closer, and now reviewable across a longer surface rather than only a one-level peek. The runtime has measured later-band pressure, stage-theme progression, rack-preserving life loss, deterministic review personas, component-derived sprites, labeled cue-window influenced audio, and a reusable Platinum audio conformance lab. The preview still needs stronger stage-five-and-beyond survivability and another human-reviewed sound/browser pass before it feels compelling.

categories.label: Audio character and Galaxian acoustic feel
metricSet: event mix density during live play
runtime cue versus promoted reference-window lab score
categories.currentRead: Cue ownership and shape are harnessed, CPU analysis finds mixed-source cue candidates, promoted windows are labeled with waveform/spectrogram previews, and the reusable Platinum audio conformance lab compares runtime synthesis against those windows. The latest pass also tightened the weakest cue family and added a dedicated stage-advance handoff so the longer surface sounds less abruptly stitched together.
categories.nextEvidenceNeeded: Human-listen the generated runtime/reference cue preview pairs, replace dirty mixed-gameplay windows, and score live playback against the target set.

categories.label: Motion pace and lower-field pressure
categories.currentRead: The latest pass extended stage-rank pressure through stages seven, nine, and eleven, preserved rack state across life loss, and turned stage-five stress into an explicit review band. That makes the current weakness much clearer: lower-field collision fairness and later-band clean-clear consistency.
categories.nextEvidenceNeeded: Use browser-reviewed runtime traces beside reference tracklets, then tune stage-five-and-beyond dive fairness and shot cadence until later-band review personas can convert stress into clears more consistently.

categories.label: Graphic alignment and alien/player likeness
categories.currentRead: Sprites are component-derived and now live inside a longer-surface shell story: later stages can change accent and atmosphere instead of staying visually flat. That makes the preview read more like a second game than a static shell card.
categories.nextEvidenceNeeded: Human-review the component crop PNGs and runtime playfield scale, then adjust any sprite or stage-surface detail that still feels too dense or too generic.

categories.label: Platform boundary and preview safety
metricSet: Platinum APIs only for shared shell behavior
categories.currentRead: The preview remains cleanly application-owned while using Platinum as the shell/platform layer, even as longer-surface review and personas become more serious.
categories.nextEvidenceNeeded: Keep boundary gates mandatory as the gameplay slice becomes more complete.

categories.label: Long-surface readiness and persona utility
categories.currentRead: The preview now has a real longer-surface review story: a professional-strength review harness can hold the game long enough to expose later-band problems, and stage five is explicitly measurable as a stress band rather than hidden behind one-level-only framing.
categories.nextEvidenceNeeded: Raise later-band clear consistency and persona reliability enough that the longer surface feels convincingly learnable instead of merely stress-testable.

recommendedNext: Tune stage-five-and-beyond dive collision fairness and shot cadence using the new longer-surface artifact and persona runs.
Human-listen the promoted Galaxian shot, dive, enemy-shot, life-loss, and hit cue windows after the latest scoutHit/stage-advance pass.
Browser-review the stage-theme progression and component-derived sprites at actual gameplay scale during longer review runs.
Keep Guardians beta-facing preview claims blocked until the playtest-weighted score reaches at least 7.0/10 with a stronger long-surface read.
